pages.raw_text:
Editor. Press-During the past
four months have been reading
Your paper regularly and was
pleased to note that The Press is
one paper which makes an effort
to enlighten the public regarding


true conditions in Spokane.


y Awhile ago I read with much in
terest an article dealing with the
street car service in eastern cities
Having been an easterner myself
UP to a year ago, and coming from
q city which boasted of having the
best car service In, the United
EtatesssI naturally wass somewhat
surprised to learn that the best
service - be had in Spokane ne.
fdessitated q walt of 10 minutes.
Y Now It seems to me that If Spo.
Kane boosters desire to impress the
minds. sof visitors with Spokane's
greatness, good way In which to
accomplish this would be to use
their influence with the Washing
ton Water Power Co. in an honest
endeavor to secure good service,
y. e., Ave minute service on all
Zines, and we'll heated and equipped
cars. As matter of fact, when
you consider the rapid growth OF
Spokane during the past FIVE or six
years, it will be readily seen that
such a service will soon be not only
desirable. but an absolute neces
Fsity. If the boosters are inclined to
Ioubt the ACCESSIBILITY of so doing
they need only to consider how
absurd and wholly inconsistent IL
would be for a citizen of Spokane
to try to dazzle the eyes of a per
son from st. Paul, Minneapolis
Cleveland or Chicago with the
greatness of the power city," with
it's 10, Is or 20 minute car service
when the latter Individual had been
accustomed to a three or five min
cute service all over his or her home
town.


1 The writer begs to offer a little
Suggestion. If Miss partition or an
|bther Press correspondent would
State in your paper without fear or
FAVOR the true state of affairs con
Corning Spokane's street car serv
ice, there is just possibility that
